rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JOYCE COSEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/7/20. Assessment fee was not paid, as alleged in paragraph 11 of the amended petition. Notice was given to "Merlon" Cosey ("Brother"), but the amended petition shows a "Marlon Cose, Nephew" without identifying whose child Marlon Cosey is. (Counsel's declaration shows a different total number of hours than the number of hours stated in paragraph 19 of the amended petition.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HILDA H YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/20. (Unable to send formal Notes due to technical issues with Word.) A petition for final distribution must show the net cash to be distributed to each heir. [LRSF 14.67 L.7.] If real property was sold for over $2,760,000, why is there only $1,078,495.50 on hand? And as of what date is that bank balance (all being held in one bank account in excess of the FDIC amount)?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEWTON B. BILLUPS III
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/10/20. Examiner unable to finish before 3pm deadline. Matter will go on for hearing 3/10/20 for the following issues: 1) $3,000.00 in fees were disbursed to attorney in Mississippi Thomas T. Ross without court authorization. In the third declaration the administrator states the $3,000.00 disbursed to attorney Thomas T. Ross will be deducted from the administrator's commission and retained in the estate's bank account and added to the requested reserve. 2 Court to determine whether $114,926.20 assigned to American Research Bureau to find 4 individuals in California and 1 individual is Louisiana is reasonable.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD H. LUCY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Proceeds from sale of real property were placed into a client trust account, rather than an estate account, which is not interest accruing. Petitioner alleges that this estate had no cash assets prior to 5/25/19, and the proceeds from sale were held in the client trust account to satisfy bond company restrictions. 2) Petitioner anticipates that there will be $33,000 in refunds from the Franchise Tax Board for 2019 taxes. Court to determine whether estate is ready to be closed.
